title: macOS and iOS Support, Security, and Recovery · Device Forensics
summary: A realistic macos and ios support, security, and recovery ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in Apple platform support and recovery record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish Apple hardware architecture, macOS and iOS roles, accounts, Apple Account, local users, managed identities, services, keychain, and device-management boundaries.
Use Finder, System Settings, Activity Monitor, Disk Utility, System Information, Terminal, logs, safe mode, recovery, and accessibility tools for bounded evidence.
Trace APFS volumes, snapshots, encryption, free space, iCloud state, local optimization, versions, conflicts, backup, and restore without assuming sync equals backup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Apple Platform Model
steps.instruction:
debug case — Mixed Apple silicon and Intel devices have sync conflicts, trust prompts, full storage, managed policy, encrypted recovery, and peripheral failures. The debug variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Distinguish Apple hardware architecture, macOS and iOS roles, accounts, Apple Account, local users, managed identities, services, keychain, and device-management boundaries.

Inspect the Apple Platform Model trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Apple Platform Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish apple hardware architecture, macos and ios roles, accounts, apple account, local users, managed identities, services, keychain, and device-management boundaries. For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Apple Platform Model, keep Apple Platform Model connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Apple Changed Case
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Apple Changed Case: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Mixed Apple silicon and Intel devices have sync conflicts, trust prompts, full storage, managed policy, encrypted recovery, and peripheral failures. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Apple Changed Case.
steps.why: Retrieving Apple Changed Case through the concrete Apple platform support and recovery record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Apple Changed Case, keep Apple Changed Case connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Apple Changed Case, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Apple Changed Case precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Apple Changed Case, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Mixed Apple silicon and Intel devices have sync conflicts, trust prompts, full storage, managed policy, encrypted recovery, and peripheral failures. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair.

For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Apple Changed Case, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A repair that works on one unmanaged Mac transfers unchanged to every managed Apple device.
